summary: The average time to close a DOI investigation rose from 214 days in Fiscal 2024 to 251 days in Fiscal 2025, which DOI's own reporting attributes to the mix of cases it handled that year: fewer, more complex investigations, often run jointly with prosecutors and other law enforcement partners, take longer to complete than a larger number of simpler ones. The Fiscal 2026 figure (a year-to-date average of 325 days through the first four months, per DOI's preliminary report) suggests this trend has continued.

sources.label: DOI, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing, reflecting fewer but more complex joint investigations
